intro: Pentacalia miniaurita, also known as the Pentacalia miniaurita plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Pentacalia miniaurita include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pentacalia miniaurita is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion to treat various health conditions, and is also used in modern medicine as a potential source of new therapeutic agents.
